page: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine belonging to the DCSv2 server family. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core, 1 thread, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 51 GB of local storage. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. This instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by moderate single-core capabilities and constrained multi-core or high-throughput performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance of 1895.19 and the Geekbench single-core score of 1278.0 both rank in the average tier. Similarly, single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression at 29.37 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 11.05 GB/sec achieve average marks. However, due to the single-core limitation, multi-core metrics are poor, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1332.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1895.19. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached read and write speeds of 17.16 GB/sec and 13.6 GB/sec respectively.

The primary tradeoff of the DC1s_v2 is its strict resource limitation, which restricts its utility for parallelized or memory-intensive workloads. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations fall into the lower performance tier, indicating that this server is not suited for heavy database or high-concurrency web serving. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low resource footprint positions this instance as a budget-friendly option for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic testing, or administrative utility tasks that do not require multi-threaded processing or high memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine from the DCSv2 family, designed for lightweight workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G x86_64 processor running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of RAM and 51 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1278.0 and average single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single-core, single-thread limitation. This server is highly cost-efficient for low-demand, single-threaded applications, utility scripts, and basic development environments, but it is not suitable for database hosting, high-concurrency web serving, or parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E-2288G processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 51 GB of local storage, offering average single-core performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A single-core, dedicated Intel processor instance with a balanced memory ratio, optimized for lightweight single-threaded utility workloads and basic testing.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor with 1 vCPU and 1 physical core
4.0 GB of system memory and 51 GB of local storage
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Low multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities due to single-thread limit
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic testing
